results.title: Adobe village: valley of Mexico
results.creator_credit: Joseph Brun, Louis De Rochemont and Louis de Rochemont Associates
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Earth and it’s peoples”.
Built on a swamp at a high altitude, modern Mexico City was built on the Aztec city of Teotihuacan by the Spanish who invaded the region. This documentary follows a typical Mexican family in an adobe village, weaving serapes, harvesting corn and raising cattles, sheep, horses, goats and donkeys. Discusses their diet, education, work and family life. Also attempts to explain how Aztec Indian culture has ‘blended’ with Spanish culture.

results.title: Volcanoes
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Earth”.
Evidence of high termperature and pressure beneath the earth’s surface is seen in bubbling mud pots, hot spering geysers, lava fountains and thunderous volcanic eruptions. Further effects of volcanic action on the earth’s crust are seen in cinder cones, crater lakes, calderas, shield and strato-volcanoes.

results.title: Resources in its crust
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Earth”.
The major useful organic and mineral substances found in the earth’s crust. Classifies the resources as the metallic minerals, the non-metallic minerals, and the mineral fuels. How some of them are extracted and processed. Steps necessary to conserve irreplaceable resources.

results.title: Its water cycle
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Earth”.
Shows clouds massing and evaporating, ice crystals growing under photomicrography, and air and water under varying pressures in a plastic box, in order to illustrate the hydrologic or evaporation-condensation-precipitation cycle.

results.title: Its oceans
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Earth”.
A comprehensive study, revealing the changing pattern of the ocean’s surface, the living and non-living things in the waters, the nature of the ocean floor, and the influence of the oceans on man. The roles of modern scientists - oceanographers, marine biologists and others - as they work to gain more knowledge of the earth’s vast, deep oceans.

results.title: Changes in its surface
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Earth”.
Uses the illustration of an erupting volcano to show that the surface of the earth is changing. Explains and demonstrates other forces both internal and external that are changing the earth’s surface.

results.title: Disappearing world
results.brief_description: This series reveals aspects of tribal life and customs, as well as current threats to the cultural and social identity of indigeneous peoples. Each episode is devoted to a different tribe in Papua New Guinea, Brazil, the Solomon Islands and Africa.
